title: Add distinct shareable URLs for recipes
description: Recipes (food and drink) currently aren't individually addressable via URL, so they can't be shared with others by link. Each recipe should have its own route (e.g., /recipes/:id, /cocktails/recipes/:id) that deep-links to a detail view. This enables sharing links to specific recipes and improves navigation UX (browser back/forward, bookmarks). Requires introducing a router (e.g., react-router) since the app currently uses tab-based state in App.tsx.
close_reason: Added react-router-dom. Routes: /family, /meals/{planner,templates,shopping}, /recipes, /recipes/:id, /cocktails/{suggest,bar}, /cocktails/recipes, /cocktails/recipes/:id. Detail pages use existing useRecipe(id)/useDrinkRecipe(id) hooks. Server SPA fallback already handles deep-link refresh. Verified via playwright smoke test.

title: Promote Recipes to top-level nav tab
description: Split Recipes out of Meals sub-tabs into its own top-level tab. Navigation changes from Family|Meals|Cocktails to Family|Meals|Recipes|Cocktails. Meals sub-tabs become Planner|Templates|Shopping. RecipeManager is already self-contained (no props, independent hooks/routes), so this is purely a navigation restructure in App.tsx plus doc updates to CLAUDE.md/REQUIREMENTS.md/IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md.
close_reason: Promoted Recipes to top-level nav tab in App.tsx and updated CLAUDE.md docs

title: Establish canonical heading type scale (h1/h2/h3 sizing)
description: Phase 17 (fewd-txa) verified that Playfair Display is correctly applied to all h1/h2/h3 tags via @layer base. However, sizing is inconsistent across the app: h2 splits between text-lg and text-xl without clear intent; several h3s in RecipeManager.tsx have no explicit size class. Pick a canonical scale (e.g. h1=text-2xl, h2=text-xl, h3=text-lg) and apply consistently across RecipeManager, MealPlanner, DrinkRecipeManager, FamilyManager, SettingsPanel, ShoppingList, TemplateManager, CocktailSuggester (~10 files). Pure visual pass — no behavior changes.

title: Use slugs instead of UUIDs in recipe URLs (pinned at creation)
description:
Today recipe detail URLs embed UUIDs (e.g. /recipes/abc-123-def-456 and /cocktails/recipes/abc-123-def-456). They work for sharing, but they're opaque and ugly. Switch the URL-visible identifier to a human-readable slug derived from the recipe name (e.g. /recipes/pizza-margherita, /cocktails/recipes/negroni).

Depends on: fewd-84t (routing introduced there; this extends it).
design:
## Decision: slugs pinned at creation

Slugs are generated once when the recipe is created and never updated when the recipe's name changes. This keeps shared links stable at the cost of mild staleness (URL can diverge from the displayed name). Rejected alternative: maintaining a `recipe_slugs` history table for redirects on rename — doubles the complexity and isn't needed at this project's scale.

## Backend changes

- New migration: add `slug TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE` to `recipes` and `drink_recipes`. Backfill from existing `name` with collision suffixes (`-2`, `-3`, …). Create a unique index.
- Add `server/src/services/slug.rs` (or a module-level helper) with a `slugify(name) -> String` function: lowercase, strip/normalize accents, replace whitespace/punctuation with single hyphens, trim, cap length (~80 chars). Can use a lightweight dep (`slug` crate) or hand-roll.
- `RecipeService::create` / `DrinkRecipeService::create`: compute base slug, then loop `base`, `base-2`, `base-3`, … until unique (service-side check against DB, not relying on insert-retry — cleaner).
- Route dispatch: `GET /recipes/:id_or_slug` checks whether the param matches the UUID v4 regex; if yes → lookup by id, else → lookup by slug. Same for `/drink-recipes/:id_or_slug`. All other routes (update, delete, favorite, scale, enhance, adapt) continue to use UUID so mutations have a stable key.

## Frontend changes

- `src/types/recipe.ts` / `src/types/drinkRecipe.ts`: add `slug: string`.
- `src/hooks/useRecipes.ts` / `useDrinkRecipes.ts`: `useRecipe(idOrSlug)` works unchanged because the backend now resolves either. (No dedicated `useRecipeBySlug` needed.)
- Route path stays `/recipes/:id` and `/cocktails/recipes/:id` — rename the param to `:slug` for clarity if desired, but not required.
- Update the three `navigate('/recipes/${recipe.id}')` call sites and the drink recipe card `<Link to=...>` to use `recipe.slug`.
- After create/import/adapt, navigate to the new recipe's slug.

## Why pinned at creation

- Shared links never rot.
- No redirect table, no 301/302 flow, no stale-cache concerns.
- Minor UX cost: if a recipe is renamed meaningfully (e.g. "Untitled Recipe" → "Grandma's Bolognese"), the URL still says `untitled-recipe`. Acceptable — the page renders the current name.
acceptance_criteria:
- New migration runs clean on both fresh and populated databases. All existing recipes and drink recipes get a non-null, unique slug after migration.
- Creating a new recipe assigns a slug derived from the name. Creating a second recipe with the same name gets a `-2` suffix (and `-3`, etc.).
- Opening /recipes/<slug> and /cocktails/recipes/<slug> loads the correct detail page. Refresh works (SPA fallback already in place).
- Opening /recipes/<uuid> still works for backwards compatibility with any outstanding links.
- Renaming a recipe does NOT change its slug — the URL stays stable.
- All list-page card clicks navigate using slugs.
- Frontend build, lint, tests, dprint, and Rust clippy/tests pass.
- Smoke test: open a recipe by slug in a fresh tab; rename it; verify the URL still resolves and shows the new name.
notes: Follow-up to fewd-84t. Deferred from that PR to keep scope focused on routing. See PR #7 discussion.

title: Build an MCP server for fewd
description: Expose fewd's domain (family members, meals, recipes, cocktails, shopping lists, bar inventory) via a Model Context Protocol server so AI assistants (Claude Code, Claude Desktop, etc.) can query and mutate data directly. This enables use cases like: 'plan this week's meals around what's in the pantry', 'suggest a cocktail with what's in my bar', 'add a recipe from this URL'. Open questions to resolve during design: (1) language/runtime — Rust (reuse server crate as library) vs. TypeScript (thin HTTP wrapper over existing Axum API); (2) which operations to expose as tools vs. resources; (3) auth model for multi-user scenarios; (4) transport (stdio for local, HTTP/SSE for remote).

title: Display app version in UI
description: Add the application version number somewhere visible in the UI (e.g., footer, settings, or about section) so users can easily identify which version they're running. This helps with bug reports and troubleshooting.

title: Configurable database location via Settings UI
description: Add the ability to view and change the database file path from the Settings panel. Backend: add routes to get/set DB location, validate path is writable, support copying data to new location. Frontend: add 'Database Location' section to SettingsPanel with path display, edit, validation feedback, copy option, and restart notice. See IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md Phase 14.

title: Styling facelift (Phase 17)
description: Verify the warm color migration is fully complete. Check for any remaining cold gray-* or stray blue-* classes that should be primary-*. Verify heading font (Playfair Display) on all h1/h2/h3. Verify card shadows. Run dprint check, bun run lint, bun test to confirm. See IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md Phase 17.
close_reason: Phase 17 verified. Mechanical migration was already complete (zero gray-*, zero indigo/slate/zinc/neutral; red-*/stone-*/bg-white are intentional semantic neutrals). Added: (1) .card token adoption on BarInventory + CocktailSuggester category containers, (2) SettingsPanel modal rounded-lg→rounded-xl + shadow-xl→shadow-lifted for surface consistency, (3) mixer bar-tag sky→cyan to stay inside the warm-leaning semantic palette. Heading type-scale audit filed as fewd-hsv.

title: Add custom favicon and browser branding
description: Design and add a custom favicon for the web app. Create an SVG icon (fork and knife over circular plate, sage green background), generate favicon.ico (16x16 + 32x32), favicon.svg, and apple-touch-icon.png (180x180). Add to public/ and reference in index.html. See IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.md Phase 15.
